articles.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5 with Native Multimodal Reasoning and Extended Context
articles.summary: OpenAI officially released GPT-5, featuring natively fused vision-language-audio reasoning rather than bolted-on modality adapters, alongside a 1M-token context window available at launch. Benchmarks show significant gains in complex multi-step coding, scientific reasoning, and agentic tool-use tasks, narrowing the gap with specialized domain models.

articles.title: Google DeepMind's Gemini 2.5 Pro Tops Agentic Coding Benchmarks
articles.summary: Google DeepMind's Gemini 2.5 Pro model achieved state-of-the-art results on SWE-Bench Verified and HumanEval-Agent, demonstrating an ability to autonomously navigate large codebases, write tests, and submit working pull requests. The model's improved planning and self-correction loops mark a step change in practical AI-assisted software engineering.

articles.title: Meta Releases Llama 4 Maverick and Scout Models Under Open License
articles.summary: Meta released the Llama 4 family, including the 'Maverick' 400B mixture-of-experts model and the smaller 'Scout' variant optimized for on-device inference. Both models ship under a permissive license and compete directly with closed frontier models on reasoning benchmarks, further accelerating the open-weight AI ecosystem.

articles.title: Figure AI's Helix Humanoid Begins Pilot Deployment at BMW Spartanburg Plant
articles.summary: Figure AI announced that its Helix humanoid robot has started a paid pilot program at BMW's Spartanburg, SC manufacturing facility, performing bin-picking, parts transfer, and quality inspection tasks alongside human workers. The deployment represents one of the first commercial-scale uses of general-purpose humanoids in automotive manufacturing.

articles.title: Boston Dynamics Unveils Electric Atlas SDK for Third-Party Developers
articles.summary: Boston Dynamics opened a public SDK and simulation environment for its electric Atlas humanoid platform, enabling external developers to build and test manipulation and locomotion behaviors in a digital twin before deploying on hardware. The move signals a shift toward a platform-ecosystem strategy similar to what worked for Spot.

articles.title: TSMC Begins Volume Production of 2nm N2 Process Node
articles.summary: TSMC confirmed the start of high-volume manufacturing on its N2 (2nm-class) process using gate-all-around nanosheet transistors, with Apple and Nvidia among the first customers. The node delivers roughly 15% speed improvement and 30% power reduction over N3E, critical for next-generation AI accelerator and mobile SoC designs.

articles.title: Anthropic Introduces 'Constitutional Classifiers' to Harden AI Safety at Scale
articles.summary: Anthropic published research on Constitutional Classifiers, a technique that uses layered AI-generated rule sets to detect and refuse adversarial jailbreak attempts with significantly lower false-positive rates than previous guardrail methods. Early results on Claude deployments show a 85% reduction in successful prompt injection attacks while maintaining helpfulness on benign queries.

articles.title: Helion Energy Achieves Sustained Fusion Plasma Milestone Ahead of Schedule
articles.summary: Helion Energy reported that its Polaris prototype sustained a fusion plasma at over 100 million degrees Celsius for a record-breaking duration, moving the company closer to its goal of grid-connected fusion electricity by 2028. The achievement bolsters confidence in the field-reversed configuration approach and its commercial viability for clean baseload power.
